CREATE TABLE publishers (id INT PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(255));
CREATE TABLE subscriptions (id INT PRIMARY KEY, publisher_id INT, subscriber_id INT);
CREATE TABLE subscribers (id INT PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(255));
INSERT INTO publishers (id, name) VALUES (1, 'Publisher A');
INSERT INTO publishers (id, name) VALUES (2, 'Publisher B');
INSERT INTO subscribers (id, name) VALUES (1, 'Subscriber A');
INSERT INTO subscribers (id, name) VALUES (2, 'Subscriber B');
INSERT INTO subscriptions (id, publisher_id, subscriber_id) VALUES (1, 1, 1);
INSERT INTO subscriptions (id, publisher_id, subscriber_id) VALUES (2, 1, 2);
INSERT INTO subscriptions (id, publisher_id, subscriber_id) VALUES (3, 2, 1);
INSERT INTO subscriptions (id, publisher_id, subscriber_id) VALUES (4, 2, 2);
SELECT * FROM subscriptions WHERE publisher_id IN (SELECT id FROM publishers WHERE name LIKE 'Publisher%');
SELECT * FROM subscriptions LEFT JOIN publishers ON subscriptions.publisher_id = publishers.id WHERE subscribers.id = 1;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM subscriptions WHERE subscriber_id = (SELECT id FROM subscribers WHERE name = 'Subscriber A');
CREATE TABLE logs (log_id INT PRIMARY KEY, subscription_id INT, status VARCHAR(50));
INSERT INTO logs (log_id, subscription_id, status) VALUES (1, 1, 'synced');
INSERT INTO logs (log_id, subscription_id, status) VALUES (2, 2, 'pending');
SELECT * FROM logs WHERE subscription_id IN (SELECT id FROM subscriptions WHERE subscriber_id = 1);
